tidyfault 0.0.0.9
  • Reference

    gate_or() Function

    gate_or.Rd

    This function produces the OR gate polygons for fault trees.

    gate_or(size = 2, res)

    Arguments

    size

    (Default = 2) Numeric value controlling the base size/diameter of the OR gate shape. The shape is scaled proportionally. Default is 2.

    res

    (Required) Numeric value describing the resolution, or number of line segments used to draw the curved portions of the gate shape. Higher values create smoother curves. Typically set to 50 or higher.

    Value

    A data.frame (tibble) with columns x and y containing coordinate pairs that define an OR gate polygon shape. The coordinates are centered at the origin (0, 0) and represent a curved shape with a concave top and convex bottom, characteristic of OR gates in fault tree diagrams. The shape is symmetric and can be translated and scaled as needed.

    Details

    This function generates the geometric shape for OR gates in fault tree visualizations:

    • Creates a curved shape with a concave top edge and convex bottom edge

    • Uses square root functions with different scaling factors for the top and bottom curves

    • Combines two curve segments (top and bottom) to form the complete gate shape

    • Centers the shape at the origin (0, 0) for easy translation

    • The shape follows standard fault tree analysis conventions where OR gates are represented as curved shapes

    The OR gate shape uses different mathematical curves for the top (concave, using 1/size * sqrt(x)) and bottom (convex, using size * sqrt(x)) to create the distinctive curved appearance. The two curve segments are combined and centered to form the final polygon.

    Contents

    Developed by Timothy Fraser, Jingyao Tong.
